This rail-transport related article is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Adhesive_weight&action=edit).

is the weight on the driving wheels of a [locomotive](/wiki/Locomotive "Locomotive"), which determines the frictional grip between wheels and rail, and hence the drawbar pull which a locomotive can exert.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

McClellan, George B. (November 21, 1854). "Memoranda on Railways". [*Reports of Explorations and Surveys to Ascertain the Most Practicable and Economical Route for a Railroad from the Mississippi River to the Pacific Ocean*](https://books.google.com/books?id=LshOAAAAcAAJ). Vol. 1. p. 116.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Weight on the driving wheels of a train

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **relies largely or entirely on a [single source](/wiki/Wikipedia:Articles_with_a_single_source "Wikipedia:Articles with a single source")**. Relevant discussion may be found on the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:Adhesive_weight "Talk:Adhesive weight"). Please help [improve this article](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Adhesive_weight&action=edit) by [introducing citations to additional sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). *Find sources:* ["Adhesive weight"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Adhesive+weight%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Adhesive+weight%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Adhesive+weight%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Adhesive+weight%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Adhesive+weight%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Adhesive+weight%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(September 2020)* |

**Adhesive weight** is the weight on the driving wheels of a [locomotive](/wiki/Locomotive "Locomotive"), which determines the frictional grip between wheels and rail, and hence the drawbar pull which a locomotive can exert.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
